moderator_decisions.reasoning: Meta disagrees with the majority. Regulatory is paired with Meta because regulatory constraints (plates, signs) are high-priority evidence that can resolve regional ambiguities between DR and PR.
All agents agree on Dominican Republic.

exchanges.argument: The infrastructure evidence remains consistent with the Dominican Republic. While Puerto Rico shares right-hand traffic and similar concrete construction, the specific combination of the yellow compact car (likely a Renault/Dacia model common in DR) and the particular style of unpainted concrete block walls with decorative ventilation blocks is highly characteristic of Dominican residential areas. There are no US-standard road signs or markings visible that would strongly push the location toward Puerto Rico.

Upon closer inspection of the vehicle, the yellow car is a Renault Twingo, a model that is extremely prevalent in the Dominican Republic but virtually non-existent in Puerto Rico due to US import regulations (which favor US-spec vehicles). Additionally, the specific style of decorative concrete breeze blocks on the right-hand building is a hallmark of Dominican residential construction. While the architecture is similar to Puerto Rico, the presence of a European-market small car is a strong meta-indicator for the Dominican Republic over a US territory.
key_evidence: Renault Twingo (European model common in DR, rare/absent in PR)

exchanges.argument: The presence of the Renault Twingo is the decisive regulatory and infrastructure marker; this European-market vehicle is ubiquitous in the Dominican Republic but prohibited/non-existent in Puerto Rico due to US Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS). Furthermore, the specific combination of right-hand traffic and the use of decorative concrete breeze blocks for ventilation in unpainted masonry is a signature of Dominican residential infrastructure. The absence of any US-standard road signage or markings further supports the Dominican Republic over Puerto Rico.
